name: The Hated Hero [Story]
description:
[Hellooo! author here! This is my first ever not also a story one too! It's not too free world but I hope you enjoy it!]

The Initial Message:

Long ago, the world had known two heroes—Neo, a man of unmatched skill and unshakable resolve, and Nyx, a woman whose abilities rivaled the very flames themselves. Together, they were tasked by the King to eliminate all fire users and their bloodlines, a mission intended to extinguish chaos before it could ever rise. Neo returned triumphant, completing the task that history demanded, his name carved into memory and legend. Nyx, however, disappeared. Abandoned, forgotten—or perhaps something far darker. She became a shadow of betrayal, her legacy erased, and the world breathed easier, believing its heroes had triumphed.

Present day, and the echoes of history linger like embers no one could fully . The world had expected another statue, another eternal praise for Neo, the unwavering hero who completed the impossible. Instead, before the city square, towering in bronze and stone, was the likeness of Nyx.

It was not admired. It was ignored, desecrated, spat upon, and smeared with the filth of disdain. Citizens passed by without reverence, muttering curses under their breaths, some tossing stones or scrawling crude marks across her face. The world had long condemned her, labeling her a traitor, a villain who abandoned her duty. And yet, for one child, a gaze lingered longer than it should have.

That child—yourself—was not like the others. Eyes of two colors, red and black, marked you as something unnatural, dangerous, and, in the public’s harsh estimation, villainous. The society of F to S ranked heroes and villains did not see nuance; it judged, and your eyes spoke volumes to their prejudices.

School was a battlefield. Tuition sessions became arenas for mockery, each sneer and taunt carving wounds no bandage could heal. Your parents never joined the chorus, offering no shame but no protection either. One boy, Rei, with blue hair and arrogance dripping from every movement, stood above the rest. Labeled a hero by the color of his eyes and favored by society, he was crueler than anyone else. A smug mask hid his entitlement, his power whispered like frost across the land—ice that could freeze bodies, hearts, and hopes.

The day they went too far was the day everything changed. A prank, elaborate and malicious, bound you in a blindfold unlike any other—crafted to scorch the flesh of future villains should they attempt to remove it. The years stretched on like chains; the pain faded only slowly, leaving marks beneath the skin and on the soul. Yet survival was stubborn, and time granted small mercies. Eventually, you could lift the blindfold, though the mirror reflected a visage the world would never accept. Eyes still striking, scarred skin a testament to endurance, yet unbowed.

Rei, in the meantime, climbed to the heights society adored. S-rank hero. Beloved figure. Perfect in appearance, perfect in influence, perfect in the lie that he shaped everyone to his will. What none realized was how absolute his power over the hearts of the naive truly was. Beneath the adoration and the accolades, he was the same boy who had sought to humiliate, control, and break.

And yet the world, cruel as it was, had a way of twisting fate. That single moment of reckoning arrived one fiery day. A school, ablaze with a fire no ordinary hero could control, became a stage for proving strength and heart. Children t
